title: Star Wars Missions 14: The Monsters of Dweem
content:
{{Top|rwm|reb|italics=1}} {{Book |image=[[File:Missions14.jpg]] |title=''Star Wars Missions 14:
The Monsters of Dweem'' |author=[[Dave Wolverton]] |cover artist= |illustrator= |editor= |publisher=[[Scholastic]] |early release= |release date=[[October]] [[1998]]''[[Star Wars Missions 14: The Monsters of Dweem]]'' |intl release= |rereleased= |media type=Paperback |pages=68 |isbn=0590235109 |isbn2= |isbn3= |timeline=c. [[0 ABY/Legends|0.5]]–[[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]]''[[Star Wars Missions 13: Prisoner of the Nikto Pirates]]'' and the next three ''Star Wars Missions'' books tell a continuous story that is set after the Evacuation of Yavin, during the construction of [[Echo Base/Legends|Echo Base]], thus placing the books between [[0 ABY/Legends|0.5]]–[[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]] per ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''. |series=''[[Star Wars Missions]]'' |preceded by=''[[Star Wars Missions 13: Prisoner of the Nikto Pirates]]'' |followed by=''[[Star Wars Missions 15: Voyage to the Underworld]]'' }} '''''Star Wars Missions 14: The Monsters of Dweem''''' is the fourteenth adventure book in the ''[[Star Wars Missions]]'' young-reader series and [[roleplaying game]]. It was published by [[Scholastic]] in [[October]] [[1998]]. ==Publisher's summary== ''The monsters of Dweem do not take any prisoners. They are the color of ice, and can appear at any moment with their jagged teeth bared. You are an Imperial soldier who has traveled to Dweem in search of Han Solo and his Rebel friends. You will be lucky to get off the planet alive. Your mission: to trap the Rebels before the monsters of Dweem trap you.'' ''Good luck.'' ==Plot summary== The [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']], set on a course for [[Dweem]], is diverted, thanks to the machinations of [[B-1D4]]. The Captain, [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]], is outraged and powers Wondeefor down, much to the angst of [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]] and [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]]. Meanwhile, on the ''[[Zaker Besar]]'', [[Grand Moff/Legends|Grand Moff]] [[4-8C]] searches for the ''Falcon''. Instead, his crew find the remains of the ''[[M'hendosat]]'' and a [[Escape pod/Legends|life pod]] carrying the former Captain, [[Kassihm]]. [[Burra Stone]], Imperial interrogator, is abruptly awoken from his sleep on the ''Zaker Besar'', and summoned into 4-8C's presence. Meanwhile, Kassihm's pod is captured by the Imperials. Stone interrogates Kassihm, and determines that Solo was headed for Dweem. Kassihm also convinces Stone and 4-8C that he is more valuable alive, and that he would be willing to join the hunt for the Corellian. One of the Imperials, in an [[All Terrain Scout Transport/Legends|AT-ST]], leads a team through the Dweem wilderness. Encountering an ancient auto cannon, the Imperials wonder whether there really is a Rebel base on the planet. Venturing into a cave, the Imperials are set upon by [[Dweemons]], large [[spydr]]-like creatures. Though they fend off the monsters, they then encounter an avalanche. Venturing further into the cave, and encountering more Dweemons, the Imperial is forced to abandon the AT-ST, and traverse on foot. Eventually, the Imperial encounters an [[Iron Knight]], but defeats it. Realizing that the base is in fact an ancient Republic installment, they begin to make their escape, but encounter more knights. The Knights provide riddles, but the Imperial solves them, and meets back up with the group. ==Appearances== {{Mediacat|from=1|imagecat=Images from Star Wars Missions 14: The Monsters of Dweem}} {{App
